player_utterance: You don't have to talk. I just didn't want you standing out here alone.

believer_state_after.posture: standing near the bench, paper cup in hand, glancing sideways at the new arrival

player_utterance: i just came here to pay respect to the dead, and i felt a grief pressure coming from you, so i wanted to come to talk, so you don't feel alone. btw my name is Annie

believer_state_after.posture: standing near the bench, lowering the cup, turning a little more toward her

player_utterance: i came to pay respect, you don't know when you will be here... i came to talk to you because i feel that you lost someone or something, and you can't resolve it in yourself yet.and i feel Whoever you lost — they were younger than you ever expected to bury.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, hands resting on his knees, looking past her toward the candle

player_utterance: Tell me about him. Not the funeral. Him.im a psychologist, this may answer some of the question in your head... you said what you lost is hard to name, im sorry if im asking a lot from you, but what is it, what stopped you from saying amen, please tell me

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, elbows on knees, looking down at his folded hands

player_utterance: is it the money was what made you silent? in such a economy this is a hard test for a believer's faith, if you are one of course, or a normal human being, stading on something will cost you limbs....so what did the pastor say that you couldn't agree with? is what you know held it down?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, the program flat on his knee, turning his wedding ring with his thumb

player_utterance: so you are lutherian! that is really awesome, excuse my excitement... what a meaningful verse you recieted, don't you think holding yourself to the verses that are in your mind is putting burden on you? i felt like you are trying to live withing the verse but you can't, and that might hold you down?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, turning slightly toward her, hands open on his knees

player_utterance: before i tell you if it makes sense, you said your silence is the thing that crushes you, silence that you didn't stand up in church you mean? we all have certain limits that we can't break, and of course the lord knows that, lets look for the brighter side, atleast it didn't set well with you, you heart didn't feel easy when you heart that, you still a good member of this community john...or you have another perspective of what is going on?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, leaning forward, elbows on knees, looking at the candle

player_utterance: You don't have to make it make sense for me. It's enough that you carry it.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, hands loose in his lap, looking at her now instead of the candle
